\documentclass[11pt]{article}
\usepackage{amsmath,hyperref}
\newcommand{\vect}[1]{\mathbf{#1}}
\begin{document}
% Commands, Unicode text, and an astral symbol: π, café, 🚀.
\section{A small \LaTeX{} example}\label{sec:intro}
This is \emph{emphasized}, \textbf{bold}, and \texttt{literal}.
See \url{https://example.test} and \cite[p.~7]{knuth:1984}.
Inline math $e^{i\pi}+1=0$ and \(\vect{x}\cdot\vect{y}\) share a line.
\[
  \int_0^1 x^2\,dx = \frac{1}{3}.
\]
\begin{itemize}
  \item Refer back to Section~\ref{sec:intro}.
\end{itemize}
\verb|raw % { text }| is not a comment.
\begin{verbatim}
\command{not highlighted} % still raw 🚀
\end{verbatim}
\begin{minted}{asy}
draw((0,0)--(1,1)); // source.asy is intentionally unavailable
\end{minted}
\end{document}
